---
title: "Quest 12: Complexity - Why Speed Matters"
subtitle: "Compare Simple Big-O Patterns"
format:
  live-html:
    code-tools: true
---

::: {.quest-badge}
⚡ QUEST 12 | Difficulty: Beginner | Time: 8 minutes
:::

::: {.tip-box}
**💻 Practice Options:**
- 🦀 [Open Rust Playground](https://play.rust-lang.org/)
- 📓 [Open Notebook](../jupyterlite/content/12-complexity.ipynb)
- 📥 [Download Notebook](../files/lessons/12-complexity.ipynb)
:::

## 📖 Introduction
Some approaches scale better than others as data grows.
The goal here is intuition, not memorizing symbols.

## 💡 Example Comparison

```rust
fn linear_search(data: &[i32], target: i32) -> bool {
    for item in data {
        if *item == target {
            return true;
        }
    }
    false
}

fn main() {
    let values = vec![3, 8, 12, 20, 44];
    println!("Found 12? {}", linear_search(&values, 12));
}
```

## 🎯 Gentle Challenge
Explain why checking every item is $O(n)$.
Write your explanation in one or two plain-language sentences.

## ✅ One Possible Solution

The loop may inspect each element once in the worst case, so time grows roughly with the number of items.

::: {.quest-complete}
### ✨ Quest 12 Complete
You connected algorithm steps to growth rate.
Next: [Quest 13: Iterative Loops](13-iterative-loops.qmd)
:::
